---
title: 'Define: Codependency'
date: '2023-09-25T01:07:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'The Freedom Center'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7702853859333670376'
blogger_filename: '/2023/09/define-codependency.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/The Freedom Center/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Codependency'
---

<p>&nbsp;</p><h1>Codependency</h1>
<p>Codependency is a behavioral condition in a relationship where one person enables another person's addiction, poor mental health, immaturity, irresponsibility, or underachievement. Initially identified in the context of families dealing with alcohol or substance abuse, the term has been expanded to describe a range of dysfunctional relationship dynamics.</p>
<h3>Primary Diagnostic Criteria:</h3>
<ol>
<li><strong>Excessive Caretaking</strong>: A tendency to take on the responsibility for the feelings, needs, and lives of others, often at the expense of one's own needs.</li>
<li><strong>Need for Control</strong>: Particularly control over other people's behavior, often stemming from a desire for predictability and security.</li>
<li><strong>Low Self-Esteem</strong>: A perpetual sense of worthlessness or inadequacy that often drives the need to care for or control others.</li>
<li><strong>Dependency</strong>: Emotional or psychological reliance on the other person, leading to a fear of abandonment.</li>
<li><strong>Denial</strong>: Ignoring or refusing to acknowledge the dysfunctional or abusive aspects of the relationship.</li>
</ol>
<h3>Additional Criteria:</h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Conflict Avoidance</strong>: A tendency to go to great lengths to avoid disagreement or confrontation, even if it compromises personal values or needs.</li>
<li><strong>Lack of Boundaries</strong>: Difficulty establishing and maintaining personal boundaries, leading to unhealthy relationships.</li>
<li><strong>Reactivity</strong>: Highly sensitive emotional responses, often driven by the behavior or feelings of others.</li>
</ul>
<h3>Implications:</h3>
<ol>
<li><strong>Emotional Health</strong>: Chronic codependency can lead to emotional stress, anxiety, and depression.</li>
<li><strong>Relationship Strain</strong>: Codependent behaviors can damage relationships, leading to a cycle of dysfunction.</li>
<li><strong>Limited Personal Growth</strong>: Individuals in codependent relationships may find their own development and self-actualization compromised.</li>
<li><strong>Treatment Options</strong>: Treatment typically involves individual and/or group therapy aimed at improving self-esteem, developing healthy boundaries, and learning better communication and emotional regulation skills.</li>
</ol>
<h3>Weight of Evidence:</h3>
<p>The concept of codependency has been the subject of debate within psychological and therapeutic communities. While some see it as a distinct psychological condition deserving of its own set of diagnostic criteria, others view it more as a set of behaviors that can be addressed through treating underlying issues like addiction or mental health disorders. However, it's widely acknowledged that these behaviors can lead to unhealthy, dysfunctional relationships.</p>
